entries.title: Psalm 23 (vv. 1–3)
entries.source:
v1 The Lord is my shepherd; I shall not want.
v2 He makes me lie down in green pastures. He leads me beside still waters.
v3 He restores my soul. He leads me in paths of righteousness for his name's sake.
entries.chunkText:
v1 → The Lord is my shepherd; I shall not want.
v2 → He makes me lie down in green pastures. He leads me beside still waters.
v3 → He restores my soul. He leads me in paths of righteousness for his name's sake.

entries.title: Sonnet 18 — opening
entries.source:
Shall I compare thee to a summer's day?
Thou art more lovely and more temperate:
Rough winds do shake the darling buds of May,
And summer's lease hath all too short a date.
entries.chunkText:

entries.title: Gettysburg Address — opening
entries.source: Four score and seven years ago our fathers brought forth on this continent a new nation, conceived in Liberty, and dedicated to the proposition that all men are created equal. Now we are engaged in a great civil war, testing whether that nation can long endure.
entries.chunkText:
Four score and seven years ago our fathers brought forth on this continent a new nation, conceived in Liberty, and dedicated to the proposition that all men are created equal.
Now we are engaged in a great civil war, testing whether that nation can long endure.
